steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has a parenthetical phrase interrupting a relative clause on the left, and a passive infinitive on the right that needs a modal verb. The blank must close the parenthetical with a comma and supply the missing modal 'could' before 'be easily carved.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — malleability—could: Uses a dash instead of the comma needed to close the interrupting phrase "because of its malleability," leaving the interrupter improperly set off. ✗
  • Choice B — malleability, could: Closes the parenthetical with a comma and supplies the required modal verb, making the sentence grammatically complete. ✓
  • Choice C — malleability could: Provides no punctuation to close the interrupting phrase "because of its malleability," so the interrupter is not properly set off. ✗
  • Choice D — malleability could—: Puts a dash after "could," separating the modal from "be easily carved" and leaving the interrupting phrase unclosed. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
